question: The verse 'How he entered into the house of God, and did eat the shewbread, which was not lawful for him to eat, neither for them w...' (Matthew 12:4) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And they did eat, and were all filled: and there was taken up of fragments that remained to them twelve baskets.' (Luke 9:17) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And yet I say unto you, That even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.' (Matthew 6:29) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'But after I am risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.' (Matthew 26:32) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'He saith unto them, How many loaves have ye? go and see. And when they knew, they say, Five, and two fishes.' (Mark 6:38) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'Whether is it easier to say to the sick of the palsy, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and take up thy bed, ...' (Mark 2:9) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And they said unto her, There is none of thy kindred that is called by this name.' (Luke 1:61) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'Which indeed is the least of all seeds: but when it is grown, it is the greatest among herbs, and becometh a tree, so th...' (Matthew 13:32) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'Now I tell you before it come, that, when it is come to pass, ye may believe that I am he.' (John 13:19) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'Through the tender mercy of our God; whereby the dayspring from on high hath visited us,' (Luke 1:78) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And Jesus went about all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom,...' (Matthew 9:35) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And, behold, there talked with him two men, which were Moses and Elias:' (Luke 9:30) has roots in which OT book?
explanation: Zechariah 4:11: Two anointed ones standing by the Lord of the earth typologically connects to Moses and Elijah appearing with Jesus.

question: The verse 'Then the disciples understood that he spake unto them of John the Baptist.' (Matthew 17:13) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'He is like a man which built an house, and digged deep, and laid the foundation on a rock: and when the flood arose, the...' (Luke 6:48) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'When the men were come unto him, they said, John Baptist hath sent us unto thee, saying, Art thou he that should come? o...' (Luke 7:20) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'Every valley shall be filled, and every mountain and hill shall be brought low; and the crooked shall be made straight, ...' (Luke 3:5) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'This title then read many of the Jews: for the place where Jesus was crucified was nigh to the city: and it was written ...' (John 19:20) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And it came to pass, that, as soon as the days of his ministration were accomplished, he departed to his own house.' (Luke 1:23) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And it shall turn to you for a testimony.' (Luke 21:13) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And straightway the fountain of her blood was dried up; and she felt in her body that she was healed of that plague.' (Mark 5:29) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'Now Elisabeth’s full time came that she should be delivered; and she brought forth a son.' (Luke 1:57) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And it came to pass, that, when Elisabeth heard the salutation of Mary, the babe leaped in her womb; and Elisabeth was f...' (Luke 1:41) has roots in which OT book?
explanation: Psalms 139:13: God's intimate knowledge and formation of the unborn establishes the theological basis for prenatal spiritual awareness.

question: The verse 'And he saith unto him, Out of thine own mouth will I judge thee, thou wicked servant. Thou knewest that I was an austere...' (Luke 19:22) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'Now from the sixth hour there was darkness over all the land unto the ninth hour.' (Matthew 27:45) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'He that hath the bride is the bridegroom: but the friend of the bridegroom, which standeth and heareth him, rejoiceth gr...' (John 3:29) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And the devil said unto him, All this power will I give thee, and the glory of them: for that is delivered unto me; and ...' (Luke 4:6) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And as soon as he had spoken, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was cleansed.' (Mark 1:42) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'Ye fools and blind: for whether is greater, the gift, or the altar that sanctifieth the gift?' (Matthew 23:19) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And immediately the king sent an executioner, and commanded his head to be brought: and he went and beheaded him in the ...' (Mark 6:27) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'But when Jesus perceived their thoughts, he answering said unto them, What reason ye in your hearts?' (Luke 5:22) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And he went and joined himself to a citizen of that country; and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.' (Luke 15:15) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'The Pharisee stood and prayed thus with himself, God, I thank thee, that I am not as other men are, extortioners, unjust...' (Luke 18:11) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'They that were foolish took their lamps, and took no oil with them:' (Matthew 25:3) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'For this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found. And they began to be merry.' (Luke 15:24) has roots in which OT book?
explanation: Isaiah 62:5: God rejoices over His people as a bridegroom rejoices over his bride, depicting divine celebration.

question: The verse 'So likewise ye, when ye shall have done all those things which are commanded you, say, We are unprofitable servants: we ...' (Luke 17:10) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse '¶ And it was at Jerusalem the feast of the dedication, and it was winter.' (John 10:22) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'But if we shall say, Of men; they feared the people: for all men counted John, that he was a prophet indeed.' (Mark 11:32) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'He answered them, I have told you already, and ye did not hear: wherefore would ye hear it again? will ye also be his di...' (John 9:27) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And he saw also a certain poor widow casting in thither two mites.' (Luke 21:2) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And as he went out of the temple, one of his disciples saith unto him, Master, see what manner of stones and what buildi...' (Mark 13:1) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And laid it in his own new tomb, which he had hewn out in the rock: and he rolled a great stone to the door of the sepul...' (Matthew 27:60) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'No, nor yet Herod: for I sent you to him; and, lo, nothing worthy of death is done unto him.' (Luke 23:15) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse '¶ And there shall be signs in the sun, and in the moon, and in the stars; and upon the earth distress of nations, with p...' (Luke 21:25) has roots in which OT book?
explanation: Isaiah 13:9: Isaiah describes darkened sun, moon, and stars as signs of God's judgment on the Day of the Lord, establishing the prophetic pattern Jesus references.

question: The verse 'Then said Pilate unto them, Take ye him, and judge him according to your law. The Jews therefore said unto him, It is no...' (John 18:31) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And when they were come into the ship, the wind ceased.' (Matthew 14:32) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'When he was come down from the mountain, great multitudes followed him.' (Matthew 8:1) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And when the woman saw that she was not hid, she came trembling, and falling down before him, she declared unto him befo...' (Luke 8:47) has roots in which OT book?
explanation: Psalms 107:1: The redeemed are called to declare it publicly, showing thanksgiving testimony as appropriate response to God's deliverance.

question: The verse 'And he said unto her, Daughter, be of good comfort: thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace.' (Luke 8:48) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'Then came she and worshipped him, saying, Lord, help me.' (Matthew 15:25) has roots in which OT book?

question: The verse 'And Jesus, when he was baptized, went up straightway out of the water: and, lo, the heavens were opened unto him, and he...' (Matthew 3:16) has roots in which OT book?

question: In Luke 7:32, the phrase 'we have mourned to you, and ye have not wept' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Mark 11:1, the phrase 'Jerusalem' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Matthew 23:18, the phrase 'Whosoever shall swear' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Mark 13:8, the phrase 'earthquakes in divers places' echoes which OT passage?

question: In John 6:17, the phrase 'Jesus was not come to them' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Luke 12:29, the phrase 'seek not' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Luke 6:42, the phrase 'the beam that is in thine own eye' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Matthew 12:19, the phrase 'He shall not' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Mark 13:3, the phrase 'mount of Olives' echoes which OT passage?
explanation: Prophesies the LORD's feet will stand on the Mount of Olives at the eschaton, connecting Jesus' teaching location to messianic fulfillment.

question: In Luke 19:37, the phrase 'all the mighty works that they had seen' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Luke 4:10, the phrase 'For it is written' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Mark 4:16, the phrase 'immediately receive it with gladness' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Matthew 1:10, the phrase 'Ezekias begat Manasses' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Luke 3:29, the phrase 'Which was the son of' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Luke 20:24, the phrase 'They answered and said' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Luke 24:21, the phrase 'redeemed Israel' echoes which OT passage?

question: In John 6:19, the phrase 'Jesus walking on the sea' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Luke 8:24, the phrase 'Master, master, we perish' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Matthew 24:26, the phrase 'Wherefore if they shall say unto you' echoes which OT passage?
explanation: Condemns those who say 'the Lord says' when the Lord has not spoken, addressing the authority false teachers claim for their deceptive messages.

question: In John 11:20, the phrase 'heard that Jesus was coming' echoes which OT passage?
explanation: The prophetic announcement of the King's coming to Jerusalem prefigures Jesus's arrival bringing salvation and deliverance.

question: In Mark 4:37, the phrase 'the ship, so that it was now full' echoes which OT passage?

question: In John 15:18, the phrase 'it hated me before it hated you' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Luke 4:35, the phrase 'hurt him not' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Luke 1:43, the phrase 'whence is this to me' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Luke 11:24, the phrase 'finding none' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Luke 24:13, the phrase 'that same day' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Mark 15:38, the phrase 'the temple' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Luke 3:18, the phrase 'good news' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Mark 3:21, the phrase 'they went out' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Luke 21:27, the phrase 'they see' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Luke 18:19, the phrase 'none is good, save one, that is, God' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Matthew 24:27, the phrase 'shineth even unto the west' echoes which OT passage?

question: In John 7:36, the phrase 'Ye shall seek me' echoes which OT passage?

question: In John 9:33, the phrase 'he could do nothing' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Mark 2:27, the phrase 'The sabbath was made for man' echoes which OT passage?

question: In Matthew 18:27, the phrase 'the lord of that servant' echoes which OT passage?

question: In John 18:38, the phrase 'Pilate saith unto him' echoes which OT passage?

question: In John 7:23, the phrase 'because I have made a man every whit whole on the sabbath day' echoes which OT passage?
